title: Sesi Native Brutalist Visual Charting Engine
description: A specialized library for generating high-density, static dashboard visualizations using pure HTML, CSS, and SVG without client-side JavaScript.
summary: This engine provides a suite of functions to render various data visualizations including bar charts, sparklines, gauges, pie/donut charts, heatmaps, scatter plots, radar charts, funnels, candlesticks, and treemaps. It utilizes a 'brutalist' aesthetic, characterized by high-contrast colors and clean, geometric SVG paths. The library is designed for low-latency, server-side generation of static dashboard components. It includes internal trigonometric helpers for SVG arc calculations and dynamic color-coding logic based on data thresholds.

code_examples.description: Generating a simple horizontal bar chart
code_examples.code:
let data = [{"label": "CPU", "value": 45}, {"label": "RAM", "value": 72}];
let chart = compile_bar_chart(data, "System Load");

code_examples.description: Generating a sparkline from a numeric array
code_examples.code:
let values = [10, 25, 15, 40, 30];
let spark = compile_sparkline(values, "Traffic Trends");

key_rules: All charts are rendered as static HTML/SVG strings; no client-side JavaScript is required for interactivity or rendering.
The engine uses a specific 'brutalist' color palette, primarily relying on neon greens, reds, and yellows for status indicators.
SVG coordinate systems are hard-coded for specific viewboxes, requiring data normalization within the functions.
The library includes custom trigonometric approximations (Taylor series) for sine and cosine to handle SVG path geometry.
